active_projects.description: Conference talk walking through the zed/examples/epi-study synthetic RCT demo (KAT vs TAU, adjusted OR 3.29) as an end-to-end showcase of the /epi Claude Code workflow for a mixed clinical/informatics audience

source_materials: /home/benjamin/.config/zed/examples/epi-study/ (systematic review of entire directory: README.md, EPI_ANSWERS.md, scripts/, data/, reports/consort_report.md, reports/tables/*, logs/)
forcing_data.audience_context: Mixed clinical/informatics audience. Topic: demonstrating the Claude Code /epi extension via a synthetic RCT (ketamine-assisted therapy vs treatment-as-usual for methamphetamine use disorder, N=200, primary adjusted OR=3.29 for 12-week abstinence). Conference format, 15-20 min. Balance tooling narrative, methods/CONSORT rigor, and the headline clinical finding. Emphasize reproducibility (deterministic seeds, base-R fallbacks) and the four-step /epi -> /research -> /plan -> /implement workflow.

active_projects.completion_summary: Deleted the stale agent block from settings.json (Zed Agent Panel now uses Zed's built-in default, which auto-updates with Zed releases) and rewrote docs/general/settings.md to explain the intentional omission plus document -latest alias fallback. Claude Code is unaffected (configured via independent agent_servers.claude-acp block).

active_projects.completion_summary: Task 20 completed: synthetic RCT pipeline executed successfully as a Zed R/Python/Quarto toolchain verification on NixOS. All 7 phases finished with graceful fallbacks (base R only, no Quarto). Primary deliverable is the prioritized configuration gap log (logs/config_gaps.md) identifying missing R packages (tidyverse, survival, gtsummary, mice, languageserver), Python modules (scipy, statsmodels), and Quarto, with concrete nix remediation snippets and recommended Zed settings.json additions.
roadmap_items: Install R package set (tidyverse, survival, gtsummary, broom, mice, knitr, rmarkdown, languageserver, styler, lintr) via configuration.nix rWrapper
Install Python scientific extras (scipy, statsmodels, seaborn, scikit-learn, pyarrow) via nix profile
Install Quarto (pkgs.quarto) and verify rmarkdown/jupyter engines
Register r-languageserver in Zed settings.json and verify LSP features interactively
Create project-level flake.nix for reproducible per-task R/Python environments
